item.title: What’s Going on With the IRS?
item.description: The Trump administration’s budget cuts are looming over Tax Day.
item.dc:creator: Will Gottsegen

item.title: There’s Only One Reason to Cold Plunge
item.description: The practice has its benefits. Just not the ones wellness influencers are loudest about.
item.dc:creator: Bill Gifford

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: Gruesome Fairy Tales
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: What Paul Ehrlich’s Fear of Scarcity Did to American Politics
item.description: Ehrlich’s lurid predictions of imminent planetary doom captivated the public, but they did not come true.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Anbinder

item.title: The Celebrity Clock Is Getting Rewired
item.description: Stars can stay relevant for longer than ever now.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Fattal

item.title: There’s No Way the American West Will Have a Normal Summer
item.description: This week’s record-breaking heat dome augurs badly for the months to come.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Boyle

item.title: The New Infidelity
item.description: Micro-cheating includes all sorts of internet behavior that, to many people, might just seem innocent.
item.dc:creator: Zoe Yu

item.title: Are They Still Your Friends if You Never See Them?
item.description: The friendship crisis of American men
item.dc:creator: Andrew McCarthy

item.title: TheSeinfeldTheory of Fiction
item.description: Annoying characters let us admit that we might be annoying too.
item.dc:creator: Lily Meyer

item.title: Trump Is Kicking the Economy While It’s Down
item.description: How the war with Iran could lead to a recession
item.dc:creator: Hanna Rosin

item.title: Israel Is Missing Its Big Chance in Lebanon
item.description: The Lebanese are furious with Hezbollah, but Israel keeps giving it more reasons to fight.
item.dc:creator: Hussein Ibish

item.title: Trump Is Betraying Iran’s Pro-Democracy Protesters
item.description: He toyed with their hopes, raising expectations he never meant to fulfill.
item.dc:creator: Franklin Foer

item.title: The Lesson of Tulsi Gabbard’s Flip-Flop
item.description: The existence of war skeptics in a president’s Cabinet is not a valuable indicator of how that president will act.
item.dc:creator: Conor Friedersdorf

item.title: Maybe Turning War Into a Casino Was a Bad Idea?
item.description: A disturbing new low in the Polymarket era
item.dc:creator: Charlie Warzel

item.title: Photos: Birds in Early Springtime
item.description: Recent images of some of our fine feathered friends at work and at play in the warming Northern Hemisphere
item.dc:creator: Alan Taylor

item.title: Why Britain Is Saying No to Trump’s Iran War
item.description: Alastair Campbell on the end of the U.S.-U.K. “special relationship.” Plus: Why Democrats in Congress cannot ignore their duty, andThe Director, by Daniel Kehlmann.
item.dc:creator: David Frum

item.title: The Iran War’s Next Threat Is to Food and Water
item.description: A prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz could unleash a humanitarian crisis.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Salama

item.title: A New Level of Vaccine Purgatory
item.description: A judge suspended the CDC’s vaccine advisory committee but also said that the agency should be consulting it before making recommendations.
item.dc:creator: Katherine J. Wu

item.title: ‘I’m Far Angrier’
item.description: Can Cory Booker, once the candidate of love, run for president and stay true to who he is?
item.dc:creator: Russell Berman
